title: Unprivileged podman should allow to not use slirp4nets ans still have port forwarding
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind feature

**Description**

It looks like, now Podman is using Rootlesskit to implement port forwarding. This is confirmed by the fact, that incoming packets are seen on loopback interface, not on tap0. So, if I understand correctly, for some containers slirp4netns is not needed at all, e.g. for services which doesn't need to have network connectivity, and which could be set to listen on loopback.

comments.body: Unfortunately, no. I also hoped that it will work, but it doesn't. Two things are not quite right - loopback interface is not up, and it looks like no process is listening for port in host.
A friendly reminder that this issue had no activity for 30 days.
@ya-isakov Are you still seeing this issue?  I believe --net=none should solve the issue in that the processes in the container will be on the network namespace of the host.

@rhatdan as I said in https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues/5362#issuecomment-593166903, I've tried with --net=none, and it was not what I mean, as loopback is there but it's not up, which prevents my daemon from starting, as it was not found interfaces to listen on. Also, in host there is no process (containers-rootlessport) which is listening for my connections.
P.S. I've checked latest release - 1.8.2
Since I have not heard back, I think this solved your issue.
